coverage.note: This is a minimal reviewed vertical slice, not comprehensive coverage. Areas without records should not be interpreted as historically inactive.
partialRegions: Other regions of the Achaemenid Empire

collections.description: A reviewed Granicus-centered entry point into Alexander's 334 BCE crossing from Europe into Asia.
collections.editorialNotes: Public membership contains reviewed historical records only. Synthetic compatibility fixtures remain available outside this collection.

sourceRefs.note: Supports the collection's Granicus-centered editorial scope.

collections.description: Internal synthetic collection retained to verify pipeline links and compatibility.
collections.editorialNotes: Internal compatibility fixture; not shown on the public Collections landing page.

disputedNotes: Arrian presents a late-day contested crossing; Diodorus presents a dawn crossing with a materially different tactical sequence.
Modern critical synthesis considers detailed reconstruction and numerical totals unreliable; forces and losses are therefore omitted.
Darius III was not present and is not listed as a participant.

sourceRefs.locator: Full article, especially topography and source criticism

events.summary: In 334 BCE Alexander's invading Macedonian-led army defeated Achaemenid regional forces at the Granicus. The exact site, tactical sequence, force totals, and losses are not asserted as settled.

uncertainty.note: The representative point follows Getty TGN 6002329; it is not an exact battlefield coordinate.

people.summary: King of Macedon from 336 to 323 BCE; he led the 334 BCE crossing into Asia and fought at the Granicus.
Last Achaemenid king, ruling from 336 to 330 BCE. He was not present at the Granicus battle in this dataset.

uncertainty.note: The birth year is approximate and the surviving narrative evidence is predominantly Greek.
